text: The broader crypto market in April 2026 shows mixed signals with Bitcoin around $78,000 and Ethereum down 4%. Hyperliquid's token $HYPE is among the top gainers despite recent volatility. Institutional adoption continues with new products and integrations, including USDC bridges and tokenized stocks trading. However, the market faces challenges such as recent exploits, regulatory pressures, and a general sentiment of consolidation and maturation in the crypto space.
author: elfa_2045721314229359082

text: Hyperliquid's infrastructure strategy involves deploying its flagship HYPE++ vaults independently on three chains (Arbitrum, HyperliquidX, Base) without bridging them to avoid risks associated with cross-chain bridges. Each vault trades independently, and performance is reconciled at epoch end. This design choice prioritizes security and risk management over operational simplicity, avoiding potential multi-million dollar losses from peer compromises seen in other protocols.
author: elfa_2045829976163799474

text: There is ongoing discussion and concern about potential FUD and sell-offs related to Hyperliquid and its token $HYPE, with some users suspecting exploits or hacks. Despite this, some traders view $HYPE as an attractive trade, especially in relation to Ethereum-native assets, and consider long $HYPE / short $ETH as a favorable position. The market shows volatility with $HYPE being one of the worst performing coins recently, but also attracting significant trading interest and whale activity.
author: elfa_2045939051522343398

text: The crypto perpetuals market is undergoing structural changes with decentralized exchanges (DEXs) gaining market share relative to centralized exchanges (CEXs). HyperliquidX alone accounts for 18% of Binance's perpetuals volume, up from 2% in late 2024, while Binance's volume declined 24% over 12 months. Hyperliquid's 2025 volume reached $2.95 trillion with a small team, outperforming larger competitors. This highlights Hyperliquid's rapid growth and increasing importance in the derivatives trading ecosystem.
author: elfa_2045749363410944235

text: There is a growing narrative of a major market reset and cleanup in the crypto industry during early 2026, with over 20 funded projects shutting down or winding down operations. This includes DeFi protocols, wallets, exchanges, analytics tools, gaming and NFT projects, and Bitcoin mining operations. The causes include unsustainable business models built on hype, retail investor exodus, rising costs and regulatory pressures, and capital concentration towards Bitcoin and Ethereum. The market is consolidating towards fewer, more sustainable projects with real revenue and utility. This environment impacts sentiment around altcoins including Hyperliquid and related tokens.
author: elfa_2045796009201332225

text: Hyperliquid is gaining significant attention as a centralized perpetuals trading platform with growing trading volumes and user activity. It is seen as a potential competitor to Aave and a key player in the evolving DeFi and onchain finance landscape. Despite some recent price declines and concerns about potential hacks or exploits, the platform continues to attract whales and traders, with notable leveraged positions and active hedging strategies. Hyperliquid's infrastructure includes multiple chain deployments without bridges to reduce risk, and it has a unique buyback and inflation balancing mechanism through HIP-3 token dynamics. The platform is also associated with NFT activity and is considered by some as a safer alternative to decentralized protocols due to its centralized control.
author: elfa_2046006003901333874

text: RaveDAO-linked addresses sell approximately 23 million RAVE triggering a roughly 35 percent short-term price drop According to Arkham, a blockchain analytics platform, addresses linked to RaveDAO transferred 10 million RAVE (approximately $10.57 million) and 12.996 million RAVE (approximately $13.74 million) to Bitget around four hours ago, totaling about 22.996 million RAVE (approximately $24.31 million). Following the transfers, RAVE experienced significant selling pressure, with its price dropping sharply and declining by around 35% over the past 24 hours. — link

text: INTEL: Details of the latest U.S.-Iran draft proposal, according to Amit Segal & various other journalists: – Iran commits to a 15-year enrichment suspension, with the exception of research reactors for medical isotopes. – Iran's stockpile of 60% and 20% enriched uranium would be partially converted to reactor fuel, and partially 'downblended' to a lower enrichment level. However, it won't leave Iran. – IAEA supervison on nuclear sites. – Opening the Strait of Hormuz, with the possibility of collecting tolls. – The U.S. will offer Iran phased sanctions relief. – The U.S. will unfreeze all Iranian assets currently frozen abroad, amounting to $20 Billion or more. – The U.S. will commit to a non-aggression pact with Iran that is ensured through a UN National Security Council resolution and a treaty ratified by Congress. – The U.S. will withdraw all its military forces from the Persian Gulf. NOTE: This is simply the proposal currently being discussed, neither side have yet agreed to it. Twitter

text: According to @_FORAB, a recent Ethereum Foundation grant review highlighted "Ketman," a project dedicated to hunting down North Korean hackers infiltrating the crypto industry. The team reached out to roughly 53 projects, uncovering around 100 active DPRK IT workers. These operatives typically forge Japanese IDs to land remote Web3 tech gigs. Ketman is now actively assisting crypto teams in setting up "honeypots" during the interview phase to weed out these bad actors. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: According to Lookonchain, the Kelp DAO exploit has left Aave saddled with bad debt after the attacker deposited rsETH to drain ETH. This has sparked a massive whale exodus, with over $5.4 billion fleeing the protocol in a panic. Justin Sun alone yanked 65,584 ETH (~$154M) from the platform. Driven by this mass flight, Aave's ETH utilization rate has now maxed out at 100%. Aave: rsETH on Ethereum mainnet is fully backed. Out of an abundance of caution, rsETH remains frozen across Aave V3 and V4 and exposure to the incident is capped. WETH reserves also remain frozen across affected markets including Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, Mantle, and Linea. Aave is actively validating information and assessing potential resolutions. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: struggling hard with fomo here. more than i would like to admit. thought i would be more solidified in my process by now. apparently i am not. still fighting my old foe, lack of emotional control. my mind is playing tricks on me, trying all kinds of ways to rationalize buying back in and buying more. "best narrative since months, lockout trend, tier-1 listings coming, spaceX pfp update, if this thing doesn't giga moon then the trenches are cooked anyway." seeing giga PnLs in the groupchats befuddles my decision making. making me feel like everybody is winning but me. and invokes the feeling of "i need to do smth or i will get left behind." the whole arsenal of psyops mind tricks. idk what happens with asteroid price here. it needs immediate catalysts for continuation (tier-1 cex listings, spaceX logo update). every day without catalyst risk of uPnL getting realized increases exponentially. idk what happens, i can't predict the future. can be the next Neiro or Floki, can also be the next Gork or Kekius. my edge was seeing the vision yesterday and i tried to capitalize on that to the best of my ability. thats all i can do. and i try to find ways to be at peace with that, regardless of what happens next. its hard. "one cannot judge the quality of a decision by its immediate outcome", easily said, hard to live by. am going to bed now to prevent myself from doing smth stupid, smth emotional.

text: The KelpDAO rsETH Exploit: $292M Minted From a 1-of-1 Bridge Steps of the attack: • rsETH (Kelp's liquid restaking token) can move between chains using LayerZero's messaging system. Kelp configured it so that only ONE "Decentralized Verifier Network" (DVN) which is by LayerZero Labs was needed to approve any transfer message • Since it was a single point of trust, attackers gained control of the verification key/node tied to Kelp's own deployed contract on the source chain • The sent a "fake" transfer message that claimed: "116,500 rsETH is being sent from another chain to Ethereum mainnet." (In reality, no rsETH was ever deposited or burned on the source chain) • The attacker called a function called to trigger the release directly, because the single DVN "approved" the message, Kelp's bridge contract on mainnet trusted it 100% and released 116,500 rsETH straight to the attacker's wallet • The money was draine. No real tokens came in from another chain, the attacker just got brand-new unbacked rsETH worth ~$290–293 million. Literally magic internet money • They then tried to use it as collateral on lending platforms like Aave to borrow even more real ETH
